findings.message: New Prometheus scrape job 'redis' targets 'redis-exporter:9121', but no redis-exporter service exists anywhere under infra/ (grep for 'redis-exporter' and 'redis' matches only this file — not in compose/base.yml, services.yml, observability.yml, or any overlay). The scrape target has no corresponding exporter, so this job will perpetually fail to scrape and surfaces no metrics.
findings.suggestion: Add a redis-exporter service (e.g. oliver006/redis_exporter) to infra/compose/observability.yml (and ensure it reaches prod via services.yml/observability.yml, not just dev.yml) wired to the redis instance, OR drop this scrape job until the exporter is deployed.

findings.message: New scrape job 'redis' has no matching alert rule. The alerts directory (infra/observability/prometheus/alerts/) contains only alertmanager_alerts.yml, otel_collector_alerts.yml, postgres_alerts.yml, prometheus_alerts.yml, and slo_alerts.yml — there is no redis_alerts.yml. Redis metrics will be collected but nothing will fire when redis is down or degraded.
findings.suggestion: Add infra/observability/prometheus/alerts/redis_alerts.yml with rules (e.g. RedisDown, high memory/eviction, replication lag) routed to a valid Alertmanager receiver.

findings.message: New scrape job 'redis' has no corresponding Grafana dashboard. infra/observability/grafana/dashboards/ contains only otel-collector.json, postgres.json, prometheus.json, and slo.json — no redis.json. The new surface is scraped but has no panel for visibility.
findings.suggestion: Add a provisioned redis dashboard (infra/observability/grafana/dashboards/redis.json) so the redis scrape job has at least one panel, keeping scrape/alert/dashboard coverage in parity.
